focus: A5 security & input robustness — validator entry-guard bypass, socket-path tail-symlink TOCTOU, isWithinRoot/canonicalize containment, validateFilePath coverage
head: 12de3d3a7e
noveltyJustification: 3 net-new security findings (1 P1 + 2 P2), zero refinements of prior findings; weighted new=(5+1+1)=7, weighted total=7, ratio=1.0. F-A5-01 is a distinct TOCTOU surface not previously raised; F-A5-02/F-A5-03 are new validator-DoS and fail-open-canonicalization defects. F-003 (iter 2) gained a concrete security payload but is NOT re-counted here (cross-reference only).

findingDetails.title: Socket bind path never canonicalized before listen()/chmod — symlink at socket tail is followed, bypassing the lstat unlink-hijack fence (tail-symlink TOCTOU)
findingDetails.evidence: path.resolve(options.socketPath) at :238 does not dereference symlinks; canonicalizePath (:77-99) realpaths only the existing ancestor and re-appends the raw SOCKET_FILE_NAME tail; chmodSync(socketPath,0o600) at :355 follows symlinks; the lstat fence (canUnlinkExistingSocket :163) is only consulted on the EADDRINUSE reclaim branch (:335), not on fresh bind (:308). /tmp + os.tmpdir() are allowed roots (:118-124) and /tmp is world-writable+sticky, so a same-host local attacker can pre-create the symlink tail before the daemon binds.
findingDetails.recommendation: realpath/lstat the full socket path (reject isSymbolicLink) on the fresh-bind path too, not only on reclaim; use open+fchmod no-follow for the 0o600 step; or require a daemon-owned 0700 socket dir (never bare /tmp).
findingDetails.findingClass: defect (toctou / symlink-following on privileged fs op)
findingDetails.scopeProof: socket-server.ts in-range (hardened by 3d1667dd68); line 238 path.resolve no realpath; line 355 chmod follows; lstat only at 163 gated behind EADDRINUSE 335; code-graph fork diff -q BYTE-IDENTICAL.
findingDetails.affectedSurfaceHints: fresh-bind tail canonicalization; chmod no-follow; daemon-owned socket dir contract; both shared + code-graph copies (compounds F-003).

findingDetails.title: validateFolder runs an unbounded DFS readFileSync over the entire .opencode/specs tree on every strict validation — DoS amplification
findingDetails.evidence: collectKnownSessionIds rewinds to the .opencode/specs root (:293-297) and DFS-walks the whole subtree readFileSync-ing every *.md (:302-317) with no node/time/depth cap (only node_modules/.git/scratch pruned at :311); called unconditionally for FRONTMATTER_MEMORY_BLOCK on every validateFolder (:380); validateFolder is also exported programmatically (api/index.ts:78-83), widening the trigger surface beyond the CLI.
findingDetails.recommendation: cap nodes/files/wall-clock, short-circuit once all parent_session_ids resolve, or scope the walk to the nearest packet ancestor instead of the whole specs root.
findingDetails.scopeProof: orchestrator.ts in-range; walk loop 300-319 has no count/time/depth guard; collectKnownSessionIds called on every validateFolder via line 380; programmatic export at api/index.ts:78-83.
findingDetails.affectedSurfaceHints: walk bounding; short-circuit on lineage resolution; scope to packet ancestor; ties to the api/index.ts programmatic export trigger surface.

findingDetails.title: canonicalizePath swallows realpath failures and falls back to non-canonical resolved path — fail-open weakens isWithinAllowedSocketRoot containment
findingDetails.fileLine: shared/ipc/socket-server.ts:94-98 (consumed by resolveIpcSocketPath :134-139); byte-identical code-graph fork
findingDetails.evidence: canonicalizePath wraps realpathSync.native in a bare try/catch that returns the literal path.resolve output on any non-ENOENT failure (:94-98); isWithinAllowedSocketRoot (:126-128) is the sole containment gate and prefix-compares a possibly-non-canonical candidate against canonicalized roots, so an EACCES/EIO realpath failure on a symlinked ancestor can let a candidate pass (or wrongly fail) the boundary.
findingDetails.recommendation: fail closed (reject bind) on realpath failure rather than returning the non-canonical path; distinguish ENOENT (already handled by the existence loop) from EACCES/other.
findingDetails.scopeProof: in-range; bare catch returns resolved at 96-98; isWithinAllowedSocketRoot is the only gate (126-128) consuming it at 134-135; code-graph fork byte-identical.
findingDetails.affectedSurfaceHints: fail-closed canonicalization; ENOENT vs EACCES handling; same root weakness as F-A5-01 (incomplete canonicalization).

traceabilityChecks: iteration number: JSONL has 2 iteration lines; dispatch pre-assigned run 6 (parallel slot); honored slot over JSONL-derived 3, recorded as edge case
range integrity: HEAD 12de3d3a7e base f05bdac2cf consistent with iters 1-2

provenance: socket-server hardening (F-A5-01/03) in-range via 3d1667dd68; collectKnownSessionIds (F-A5-02) in-range; path-security.ts + api/index.ts validateFolder export NOT in-range content (context only)

integrationEvidence.note: re-exports validateFolder; confirms CLI entry-guard (orchestrator.ts:462) is not the only entry. No privileged/write action gated on validateFolder().passed (only unrelated qualityLoopResult.passed found), so the bypass is by-design library reuse, not a security boundary breach.
diff -q vs shared = BYTE-IDENTICAL; F-A5-01 + F-A5-03 exist identically in both consumers, giving F-003 (iter 2) a concrete security payload.

edgeCases: iteration-number vs pre-assigned parallel slot ambiguity (resolved toward dispatch slot 6)
/tmp vs daemon-owned-0700 dir is the deciding factor for F-A5-01 severity; deployment guarantee not determinable from diff (downgradeTrigger recorded)
path-security.ts out of range — validateFilePath coverage assessed as read-only context (containment sound; socket-server inlines a weaker canonicalize instead of using it)
confirmedClean: socket-server.ts:248-260 pre-existing-dir uid/mode hardening (directory-planting attack closed; tail node not covered = F-A5-01)
socket-server.ts:147-179 canUnlinkExistingSocket reclaim fence (lstat no-follow, foreign-uid/non-socket rejected; gap is it is not run on fresh bind)
path-security.ts:18-108 validateFilePath containment (null-byte + .. rejection, dual-side realpath, path.relative per CWE-22)
ruledOut: validator entry-guard bypass via programmatic validateFolder() import as a security finding — RULED OUT (Hunter/Skeptic/Referee): no privileged/write action is gated on validateFolder().passed; it is a pure-reporting library function, the CLI guard is a CLI nicety; bypassing skips a report not a gate. BLOCKED — do not retry as a security finding.

symlink-traversal P0 in collectKnownSessionIds — ruled out: readdirSync withFileTypes does not follow dir symlinks (isDirectory false for symlink dirents); residual is the unbounded non-following walk = DoS P2 (F-A5-02)

isWithinRoot prefix-match boundary bug — ruled out: appends path.sep before startsWith (:108) so /tmp/specs-evil does not match root /tmp/specs; both inputs canonicalized; classic separator bug correctly avoided

targetsDeferred: A4∩A5 lifecycle-security overlap (iter 8): socket close() fsync/stale-socket EADDRINUSE as security surface; WAL-checkpoint-on-close exposure

adversarialSelfCheck.F-A5-01: Hunter/Skeptic/Referee run in-iteration: Hunter flagged symlink-tail follow; Skeptic narrowed to same-uid local attacker on world-writable /tmp parent and confirmed chmod-follows + fence-only-on-reclaim; Referee landed P1 (bounded to same-uid / DoS, not arbitrary privesc) with downgradeTrigger on daemon-owned-dir guarantee.
adversarialSelfCheck.entry-guard: full 3-pass ruled out as security finding (no privileged gate).

nextFocus.focusArea: lifecycle-security overlap: socket close() fsync/stale-socket EADDRINUSE durability as a security surface (couples to F-A5-01 fresh-bind vs reclaim fence asymmetry) + WAL-checkpoint-on-close exposure window
nextFocus.reason: fresh-bind vs reclaim-bind fence asymmetry (F-A5-01) couples to A4 shutdown durability: whether close() removes the socket determines which bind branch runs and whether the lstat fence protects the next start
nextFocus.productiveCarryForward: re-assert F-A5-01 in adversarial-verify (iters 14-20) with deployment-guarantee resolved; F-003 now carries concrete security payload — escalate drift-guard in A6 (iter 12)
nextFocus.requiredEvidence: socket close() fsync/unlink ordering :363-387; crash-leaves-stale-socket path; WAL-checkpoint timing in context-server.ts
